mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of elevated inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods do not precede negative 20-day returns, or the predictive alpha vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while inflation remains above 3%, restrictive monetary policy outweighs growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL remains above 300.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment during a recessionary phase signals severe output contraction that immediately depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines combined with UNRATE increases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45% during NBER-defined recessions.
